source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A point source $Q$ emits coherent light isotropically at two frequencies $w$ and $w+ \Delta w$ with equal power I joules/sec at each frequency. Two detectors $A$ and $B$ each with a (small) sensitive area $s$, capable of responding to individual photons are located at distances $l_{A}$ and $l_{B}$ from $Q$ as shown in Fig. 8.4. In the following take $\Delta \omega/\omega\ll1$ and assume the experiment is carried out in vacuum. If now the output pulses from $A$ and $B$ are put into a coincidence circuit of resolving time $\tau$, what is the time-averaged coincidence counting rate? Assume that $\tau\ll 1/\Delta \omega$ and recall that a coincidence circuit will produce an output pulse if the two input pulses arrive within a time $\tau$ of each other.
extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object representation rather than a concrete file path or URL

issues: metadata.image_paths contains a PIL image object representation instead of a concrete file path or URL

image_dependency: 题干显式引用“as shown in Fig. 8.4”，且 `requires_image=true`、`text_dominant=false`，说明图像对理解装置布局有较强作用。
问题涉及点光源 Q 与两个探测器 A、B 的空间布置和路径差，属于空间/几何关系相关任务。
资产登记中存在 1 张主图且 `registry_passed=true`，表明图像资源可用；但题干也给出了 `l_A`、`l_B` 等关键符号，所以不是绝对纯图像依赖。
multi_step: 要求从双频相干各向同性发射、单光子探测、符合电路时间分辨率 `tau` 等条件推导时间平均符合计数率，不是单步读取。
题干包含近似条件 `Deltaomega/omega << 1` 与 `tau << 1/Deltaomega`，需要在推导中联合使用近似与物理模型。
答案为含常数因子和余弦干涉项的解析式，通常对应多步光子计数/干涉推理，而非直接代入数值。
verifiability: `answer_type=short_text`，且答案是明确的封闭解析表达式，目标输出边界清晰。
答案结构包含可核验的尺度项 `tau I^2 s^2 /(l_A^2 l_B^2 hbar^2 omega^2)` 和干涉项 `cos[(Deltaomega/c)(l_A-l_B)]`，便于后续规则或符号等价检查。
`answer_present=true` 且 `registry_passed=true`，说明样本基本完整；但公式等价形式可能带来一定自动核验难度，因此不打满分。

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: A point source $Q$ emits coherent light isotropically at two frequencies $w$ and $w+ \Delta w$ with equal power I joules/sec at each frequency. Two detectors $A$ and $B$ each with a (small) sensitive area $s$, capable of responding to individual photons are located at distances $l_{A}$ and $l_{B}$ from $Q$ as shown in Fig. 8.4. In the following take $\Delta \omega/\omega\ll1$ and assume the experiment is carried out in vacuum. If now the output pulses from $A$ and $B$ are put into a coincidence circuit of resolving time $\tau$, what is the time-averaged coincidence counting rate? Assume that $\tau\ll 1/\Delta \omega$ and recall that a coincidence circuit will produce an output pulse if the two input pulses arrive within a time $\tau$ of each other.
candidate_problem_record.multi_solution_policy_rationale: 该数据集保留多解潜力评估，但不默认强推多解 agent。

extraction_notes: image field contains a PIL image object representation rather than a concrete file path or URL
metadata.reasoning_chain:
In a coincidence of resolving time $	au$, the time-averaged coincidence counting rate is
$$P=\lim_{Tightarrow \infty} rac{1}{2T}\int_{-T}^{T}dt\int_{-	au}^{	au}P_{A}(t)P_{B}(t+x)dx.$$ 
This becomes
$$ egin{aligned}
P=&\lim_{Tightarrow\infty} rac{1}{2T}\int_{-T}^{T}dt\int_{-	au}^{	au}4C^{4}\left[1+\cos\Delta\omega\left( rac{l_{A}}{c}-tight)ight]
	imes \left[1+\cos\Delta\omega\left( rac{l_{B}}{c}-t-xight)ight]dx\
=&8	au C^{4}\lim_{Tightarrow \infty} rac{1}{2T}\int_{-T}^{T}\left\{1+\cos\left[\Delta\omega\left( rac{l_{A}}{c}-tight)ight]ight\}
	imes \left\{1+\cos\left[\Delta\omega\left( rac{l_{B}}{c}-tight)ight]ight\}dt
\end{aligned}$$
where
$$C^{4}= rac{I^{2} s^{2}}{16\pi^{2}l_{B}^{2}l_{A}^{2}\hbar^{2}\omega^{2}}=C_{1}^{2}C_{2}^{2}.$$ 
After evaluating the integrals, one obtains
$$P= rac{	au I^{2} s^{2}}{2\pi^{2} l_{B}^{2}l_{A}^{2}\hbar^{2}\omega^{2}}\left\{1+ rac{1}{2}\cos\left[ rac{\Delta\omega}{c}(l_{A}-l_{B})ight]ight\}.$$

variants.rewritten_question_text: A point source $Q$ emits coherent light isotropically at two frequencies $\omega$ and $\omega+\Delta\omega$ with equal power $I$ joules/sec at each frequency. Two detectors $A$ and $B$, each with a small sensitive area $s$ and capable of responding to individual photons, are located at distances $l_A$ and $l_B$ from $Q$ as shown in Fig. 8.4. Take $\Delta\omega/\omega \ll 1$ and assume the experiment is carried out in vacuum. If the output pulses from $A$ and $B$ are put into a coincidence circuit of resolving time $\tau$, what is the time-averaged coincidence counting rate? Assume that $\tau \ll 1/\Delta\omega$ and recall that a coincidence circuit will produce an output pulse if the two input pulses arrive within a time $\tau$ of each other.

Take $\Delta\omega/\omega \ll 1$ and assume the experiment is carried out in vacuum. If the output pulses from $A$ and $B$ are put into a coincidence circuit of resolving time $\tau$, what is the time-averaged coincidence counting rate? Assume that $\tau \ll 1/\Delta\omega$ and recall that a coincidence circuit will produce an output pulse if the two input pulses arrive within a time $\tau$ of each other.
